# Customize the autogenerated source note

When `docs-location` is set, text2confl prepends a note to every published page pointing to its source file in version
control. This guide shows how to customize or disable it.

## Disable the note

Set `add-autogenerated-note` to `false` in `.text2confl.yml`:

```yaml
add-autogenerated-note: false
```

## Customize the note text

Set `autogenerated-note` to any text in Confluence Storage Format:

```yaml
autogenerated-note: 'This page is auto-generated. Edit <a href="__doc-root____file__">source</a>.'
```

Two template variables are available:

| Variable       | Value                                                      |
|----------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| `__doc-root__` | The value of `docs-location` from your config              |
| `__file__`     | Path to the source file relative to the documentation root |

The default note text is:

```html
Edit <a href="__doc-root____file__">source file</a> instead of changing page in Confluence.
<span style="color: rgb(122,134,154); font-size: small;">Page was generated from source with <a
        href="https://github.com/zeldigas/text2confl">text2confl</a>.</span>
```

## See also

- [Configuration reference](../reference/configuration.adoc) - `docs-location`, `add-autogenerated-note`,
  `autogenerated-note` options
